Ed Wood (born Edward John "Teddy" Wood; June 17, 1929 – August 10, 1976) was an American filmmaker who made more than 100 feature-length movies during his lifetime. He is considered one of the most prolific filmmakers in history. His work includes several cult classics, including "Plan 9 from Outer Space", "Species", "Bride of Frankenstein", "A Nightmare Before Christmas", and "E.T.: The Extra-Terrestrial". He also produced the 1977 film "Back to the Future II".

rows.question: Who is the maternal grandfather of Antiochus X Eusebes?

trajectory.step_text:
<think>
To determine who is the maternal grandfather of Antiochus X Eusebes, we need to trace his lineage back through his mother's side.
Antiochus X Eusebes was the son of Seleucus IV Philopator and Cleopatra II.
Seleucus IV Philopator had two sons:
1. Antiochus VII Sidetes (father)
2. Antiochus VI Ceraunus (brother)

Cleopatra II was the daughter of Seleucus I Nicator and Laodice I.

Seleucus I Nicator had one son:
1. Seleucus II Callinicus

Therefore, the maternal grandfather of Antiochus X Eusebes would be Seleucus II Callinicus.
</think>

<answer>Seleucus II Callinicus</answer>

rows.question: Who was known by his stage name Aladin and helped organizations improve their performance as a consultant?
rows.final_answer: Aladin is a protein that is named after the achalasia-addisonianism-alacrima syndrome (triple A syndrome) which occurs when the gene is mutated. It is employed in specific oocyte meiotic stages, including spindle assembly and spindle positioning. Female mice homozygously null for aladin are sterile.
